title: Joshua Korley, GStat
text:
Biostatistics · Trial methodology · Digital biomarkers
      Statistical methods for complex biomedical evidence.
      I am Joshua Korley, a Biostatistics PhD student interested in developing Bayesian, survival, causal, adaptive-trial, and functional data methods for complex clinical, imaging, wearable, and longitudinal data. My applications span neuroscience, aging, drug development, and selected oncology research.
      
        Explore my projects ↗
        View scholarly work
      
      
        LinkedIn
        Google Scholar
        ResearchGate
        ORCID
        GitHub
        Email
      
    
    
      
        
      
      
      Current focusNeuroscience · Bayesian. CausalSurvival · Clinical Trials · FDA
      CredentialASA GStat ↗
      
        
          Joshua Korley, GStat
          Biostatistics PhD student · USC
        
        
          
            
            Google Scholar
          
          
            
            ResearchGate
          
          
          
            
            ORCID
          
        
      

    
  



  
    Bayesian methods•Adaptive clinical trials•Digital biomarkers•Functional data analysis•Survival analysis•Recurrent events•Neuroscience methods•
    Bayesian methods•Adaptive clinical trials•Digital biomarkers•Functional data analysis•Survival analysis•Recurrent events•Neuroscience methods•
  



  
    Academic foundationEducationView full education details ↗
    
      2025 to presentPhD in BiostatisticsUniversity of South CarolinaBayesian methodology, survival analysis, causal inference, clinical trial design, functional data analysis, and neuroscience.
      Summer 2025Graduate Practicum in BiostatisticsUniversity of ConnecticutBiopharmaceutical Summer Academy with training in adaptive design, estimands, multiplicity, clinical-data standards, and trial simulation.
      2023 to 2025MS in StatisticsWestern Michigan UniversityGraduate Certificate in Biostatistics and thesis research in causal-survival modeling for prostate cancer.
      2018 to 2022BSc in Mathematical ScienceUniversity of GhanaActuarial Science concentration.
    
  



  
    Research profile
    
      Methods shaped by the evidence and the decision.
      
        Research on how complex evidence can be used without losing its timing, uncertainty, heterogeneity, or clinical meaning.
        Across pharmaceutical and academic research, I work with interim trial evidence, recurrent and terminal events, wearable activity, circadian signals, neuroimaging, cognitive outcomes, treatment heterogeneity, and patient-level risk. The goal is methodology that is rigorous enough for statistical research and useful enough for real biomedical decisions.
      
    
    
      01Adaptive clinical trialsBayesian and frequentist designs for interim learning, continuation, modification, stopping, sample-size revision, subgroup assessment, estimands, multiplicity, and historical borrowing.
      02Digital and imaging biomarkersFunctional and longitudinal methods that preserve timing, rhythm, variability, intensity, distributional shape, and spatial structure in wearable and neuroimaging data.
      03Survival and aging outcomesBayesian survival, competing-risk, recurrent-event, informative-dropout, and longitudinal models for outcomes shaped by comorbidity, hospitalization, and terminal events.
      04Causal and precision methodsTreatment-effect heterogeneity, propensity-score methods, inverse probability weighting, doubly robust estimation, and clinically interpretable patient-level risk.
    
    
      PhDBiostatistics
      GStatASA accreditation
      2Pharmaceutical research internships
      6+Core methodological areas
    
  



  
    
      Selected workFeatured research
      View all projects ↗
    
    
      
        01 · Statistical methodology
        Independent methodological researchOrthogonal Estimation of Recurrent Event Treatment EffectsAn estimand-driven framework for recurrent outcomes when a terminal event changes how long recurrent events can be observed.Recurrent eventsCausal inferenceView project ↗
      
      
        02 · Neuroscience
        National Alzheimer’s Coordinating Center dataTiming of First Neuroimaging in Alzheimer’s DiseaseA competing-risk investigation of when participants receive their first MRI or PET scan, with death before imaging treated as a competing event.NeuroimagingCompeting risksView project ↗
      
      
        03 · Cognitive aging
        USCWearable Activity Patterns and Cognitive ImpairmentFunctional data analysis of daily activity distributions, preserving timing, intensity, variability, and distributional shape beyond crude activity totals.Digital biomarkersFunctional dataWearables

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching and mentorship
    Making statistical ideas clear, useful, and rigorous.
    My teaching spans introductory statistics, business statistics, probability, R programming, actuarial science, and quantitative research mentorship.
  



  
    
      WMUGraduate Student Instructor · Department of Statistics · August 2023 to April 2025
    
    Led laboratory sessions, recitations, office hours, and applied demonstrations for more than 100 undergraduate students per semester. Co-developed instructional materials and laboratory modules with an emphasis on clarity, organization, and reproducible analysis.
    
      InstructorSTAT 1600 · Discovery With DataDescriptive statistics, inference, data exploration, hypothesis testing, and introductory regression.
      TutorSTAT 2160 · Business StatisticsStatistical reasoning and applied analysis for decision-making in business settings.
      TutorSTAT 3620 · ProbabilityCombinatorics, discrete and continuous distributions, and foundations of probability theory.
      Instructor supportSTAT 2600 · R Statistical Programming and ComputingData workflows, reproducible programming, visualization, and statistical computing in R.
    

    
      University of GhanaTeaching Assistant · Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science · October 2022 to December 2023
    
    Delivered tutorials, targeted review sessions, and academic support in statistics, probability, and actuarial science. Introduced R and Python assignments to strengthen reproducible data analysis.
    
      StatisticsSTAT 111 · Introduction to StatisticsDescriptive methods, statistical reasoning, and foundational data analysis.
      ProbabilitySTAT 221 · Introduction to Statistics and Probability IProbability concepts, random variables, distributions, and introductory inference.
      Statistical theorySTAT 333 · Statistical InferenceEstimation, testing, and theoretical foundations for statistical inference.
      Actuarial scienceACTU 605 · Actuarial MathematicsQuantitative methods for contingent payments and actuarial risk calculations.
      Actuarial scienceACTU 405 · Actuarial EconomicsEconomic reasoning and financial concepts for actuarial applications.
      Actuarial scienceACTU 345 · Actuarial StatisticsStatistical tools, modeling principles, and probability methods for actuarial work.
    

    
      Research mentorshipSupport beyond the classroom
      
        PhD dissertation support in mental health, May 2025. Guided confirmatory factor analysis and structural equation modeling for resilience and mental health outcomes, including data preparation, interpretation, and reporting in R.
        Graduate dissertation support, March 2024 to May 2025. Advised on quantitative study design, statistical diagnostics, natural language analysis, and dissertation reporting.

title: Projects
text:
Project portfolio
    Research projects across methods and applications.
    I develop and apply methods for clinical trials, digital biomarkers, survival and aging outcomes, causal inference, and longitudinal biomedical data. The work connects rigorous statistical questions with evidence from wearable sensors, imaging, clinical studies, and population health.
  



  
    
      A connected methods program
      Design · measurement · outcomes · decisions
    
    
      01Adaptive clinical trialsBayesian and frequentist approaches for learning during a trial, revising design features, assessing subgroups, and supporting continuation, modification, or stopping decisions while preserving valid inference.
      02Digital and imaging biomarkersFunctional and longitudinal methods for wearable activity, heart-rate, sleep-wake, circadian, MRI, and PET data that retain timing, rhythm, intensity, variability, and distributional structure.
      03Survival and aging outcomesMethods for competing risks, recurrent events, interval censoring, and informative observation processes in studies of aging, neuroimaging, hospitalization, functional change, and mortality.
      04Causal and precision methodsEstimand-centered analyses of treatment effects, treatment heterogeneity, mediation, and risk-guided care across drug development, neuroscience, oncology, and other biomedical applications.
    

    
      All
      Statistical methodology
      Neuroscience
      Bayesian modeling
      Survival analysis
      Causal inference
      Clinical trials
      Digital biomarkers
      Applied biostatistics
      Longitudinal analysis
    
    12 projects

    
      
        Statistical methodology
        Independent methodological research · 2025 to presentOrthogonal Estimation of Recurrent Event Treatment Effects with Terminal EventsAn estimand-driven framework for recurrent clinical outcomes when death or another terminal event changes how long recurrent events can be observed.Methods and scopeSurvival-standardized estimands, influence-function-based inference, covariate adjustment, analytic variance estimation, and simulation under covariate-adaptive randomization.Recurrent eventsCausal inferenceClinical trialsView project ↗Related foundational work ↗
      

      
        Neuroscience
        National Alzheimer’s Coordinating Center data · 2025 to presentTiming of First Neuroimaging in Alzheimer’s DiseaseA competing-risk investigation of when participants receive their first MRI or PET scan, with death before imaging treated as a competing event.Methods and scopeCause-specific accelerated failure time models, Cox models with time-varying effects, cumulative incidence profiles, subgroup trajectories, and hierarchical extensions.Alzheimer’s diseaseNeuroimagingCompeting risksView project ↗ADMRI-Timing tool ↗Presentation slides ↗
      

      
        Neuroscience
        University of South Carolina · 2025 to presentWearable Activity Patterns and Cognitive ImpairmentResearch on how the timing, intensity, variability, and distributional shape of daily physical activity relate to cognitive impairment among older adults in the NHATS cohort.Methods and scopeFunctional data analysis, local distributional summaries, covariate-adjusted modeling, sensitivity analysis, and processing of wearable-derived digital biomarkers beyond total activity counts.Cognitive agingDigital biomarkersFunctional data
      

      
        Bayesian modeling
        Collaboration with the University of Warwick · 2025 to presentCircadian Modeling of 24-Hour ECG SignalsSubject-specific modeling of circadian rhythm disruption and cardiac risk using 24-hour ECG recordings from the MUSIC cohort.Methods and scopeBayesian hidden Markov models, semi-Markov models, transition-probability summaries, latent-state features, and survival outcomes.Hidden Markov modelsECGCircadian rhythms
      

      
        Digital biomarkers
        ASPIRE-funded research · University of South Carolina · Starting August 2026Fitbit-Derived Circadian Digital Phenotypes in cardiology, Alzheimer’s Disease and Related DementiasResearch on wearable-derived activity, heart-rate, sleep-wake, and circadian signals as digital phenotypes of cognitive and functional change in aging and related dementias.Methods and scopeFunctional and longitudinal data analysis, circadian feature extraction, Bayesian hidden and semi-Markov models, and association of temporal patterns with cognition and Alzheimer’s disease and related dementia outcomes.ASPIREWearable dataCircadian rhythmsADRD
      

      
        Survival analysis
        University of South Carolina · 2025Robust Survival Estimation under Interval CensoringA simulation and applied assessment of distribution-free, parametric, and Bayesian approaches to survival estimation under interval censoring.Methods and scopeTurnbull NPMLE, expectation maximization, Weibull accelerated failure time models, Bayesian accelerated failure time models, integrated squared error, and Brier scores.Interval censoringBayesian AFTEM algorithmRead preprint ↗
      

      
        Causal inference
        Western Michigan University · 2024 to 2025Individualized Treatment Effects in Advanced Prostate CancerA causal-survival analysis of treatment-effect heterogeneity for risk-guided therapy in advanced prostate cancer.Methods and scopeWeibull accelerated failure time modeling, treatment-by-covariate interactions, individualized time ratios, delta-method inference, and bootstrap uncertainty estimation.Treatment heterogeneitySurvival analysisPrecision medicineView project ↗arXiv preprint ↗
      

      
        Clinical trials
        University of Connecticut Biopharmaceutical Summer Academy · 2025Bayesian Adaptive Dose Finding in a Phase II Psoriasis TrialDose selection and decision reliability under realistic missing-data and intercurrent-event settings in a simulated Phase II psoriasis trial.Methods and scopeBayesian adaptive design, historical borrowing, multiplicity control, estimands, PASI75 and PASI50 endpoints, and MCAR, MAR, and MNAR sensitivity analyses.Adaptive trialsDose findingMissing dataView project ↗Conference posters ↗
      

      
        Causal inference
        Western Michigan University · 2024Heart Failure Survival Analysis with Predictive and Causal MethodsAn applied analysis of survival prediction, prognostic factors, and clinical mediation pathways in heart failure.Methods and scopeLogistic regression, random forest, support vector machines, penalized regression, and structural equation modeling.Heart failureMachine learningMediationView project ↗
      

      
        Clinical trials
        Western Michigan University · 2024Alzheimer’s Disease Clinical Trial Subgroup AnalysisAn investigation of heterogeneity in cognitive decline and treatment response using ACTC Solanezumab trial data.Methods and scopeRegression, subgroup analysis, interaction modeling, MMSE and ARIA endpoints, APOE4 status, centiloid level, education, and BMI.Alzheimer’s diseaseSubgroupsClinical trialsView project ↗
      

      
        Applied biostatistics
        Collaborative research · 2024 to 2025Forecasting Lung Cancer MortalityA comparative assessment of time-series models for forecasting lung cancer mortality in the United States.Methods and scopeSimple exponential smoothing, Holt methods, and ARIMA models.CancerTime seriesForecastingView project ↗arXiv preprint ↗
      

      
        Longitudinal analysis
        Western Michigan University · 2024Longitudinal Modeling of HAART and CD4 TrajectoriesTwo related investigations of treatment response, small samples, and unbalanced longitudinal designs in HIV research.Methods and scopeGeneralized least squares, linear mixed-effects models, GEE, GLMM, interaction modeling, and simulation across sample sizes.HIVMixed modelsLongitudinal dataView project ↗Method comparison project ↗

title: Community
text:
Community
    People who have been part of my growth.
    Growth is shaped by people who contribute in different ways and at different stages of life. This page recognizes the advisors, collaborators, managers, and mentors whose guidance and support have been meaningful along my journey.
  



  
    
      Academic guidance
      My academic mentors have guided important stages of my development in biostatistics. Their perspectives span Bayesian modeling, functional and distributional data analysis, survival methods, and high-dimensional biomedical applications.
